items.description: SLICE-0119 Research becomes a vault kind, not a separate qmd collection (ADR-0039, dissolves G2+G9). No blockers. Soft-order: prefer before the incremental-update item, both touch cli-search.test.ts. Owns the README [research] block + research_path config removal (the cleanup item does NOT touch [research]).
steps: Remove the literal `research` qmd collection registration from `wiki sync` (src/cli/verbs/sync.ts) and the research query path from `wiki search` (src/cli/verbs/search.ts), including the --include-research flag plumbing on both and in src/cli/usage.ts.
Remove the per-project `research_path` field from ProjectConfig (src/config/project.ts) and every reader (sync.ts, search.ts); drop the stale research_path header comment in src/integrations/qmd.ts.
Delete the dead `[research] sources` config example from README.md.

Confirm research files into the existing doc section's `research` bucket via the config-driven tree: `wiki create research` (or --category research) writes to docs/research/ with a DOC id and is returned by ordinary `wiki search`.
Update pinned tests that exercised --include-research / research_path to the new contract (do not silently delete; rewrite to the no-research-collection behavior). Use a TEMP vault.
Run bun run build, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test — all green — and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0120 Capture is honest on frontmatter (G1, ADR-0040 capture side). No blockers. NOTE: capture sees every write (hook passes raw path, no area filter), so this is frontmatter-only — a bare draft stays silent; the forgot-to-stamp net is item 0125's session-end reminder. Most of the warn/idempotent behavior already exists in capture.ts; the deliverable is pinning the contract with tests and ensuring an id/template draft that resolves to no kind WARNS rather than returning null.
steps: In src/artifacts/capture.ts confirm/ensure: a draft stamped `template:<kind>` resolvable against the per-vault structure files into that kind via mintAndWrite on a temp vault.
Ensure a draft carrying id: or template: that resolves to NO registered kind returns the 'authored but not captured — kind unknown' warn outcome (not null, not a write).
Ensure a write with no id and no template frontmatter returns null (silent) — document in the test why a bare draft cannot warn (capture sees every write).
Ensure re-firing capture on an already-filed (id-stamped) draft is idempotent (files once).
Add tests pinning each branch; the unknown-kind test must fail if capture regresses to returning null on an id/template-bearing draft. Use a TEMP vault.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0121 Per-project allocation lock prevents the duplicate-id race (G3, ADR-0041 lock side). No blockers. SHARED-SEAM RULE: implement the lock INSIDE mintAndWrite in src/artifacts/store.ts so it covers both create and capture without editing capture.ts.
steps: Add a short-lived per-project lockfile under <vault>/.wiki/ (or project dir), created with the `wx` exclusive flag and carrying a stale-lock timeout so a crashed writer cannot wedge the vault.
Wrap the allocate-id -> write-file critical section in mintAndWrite (store.ts) with acquire/release; release on BOTH success and error paths.

Ensure two near-simultaneous creates in one project yield distinct ids (deterministic test, not timing-flaky); a stale lockfile past timeout is reclaimed (no deadlock); creates in different projects do not contend.
Tests use a TEMP vault only.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0122 wiki doctor --fix repairs duplicate ids and spine drift (ADR-0043). No blockers. Most complex item: id renumber PLUS rewrite of all inbound [[id]] links and aliases. Reuse existing reference-collection infra.
steps: Add a --fix mode to wiki doctor (src/bootstrap/doctor.ts + src/cli/verbs/vault.ts or the doctor verb) that renumbers duplicate ids (keep one canonical, reassign the other from the section id-space) and updates inbound [[id]] links and aliases so references still resolve.
Also apply the mechanical fixes fmt --write already does.
Ensure doctor WITHOUT --fix stays detect-only (no writes); a vault with two PRD-0005 files is repaired to distinct ids; a second --fix run is idempotent.
Tests cover the link-rewriting path, not just the renumber; use a TEMP vault.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0123 doctor --setup honest about non-Pi subagent capture reach (G7, ADR-0043). No blockers. The empirical 'does Codex/Claude PostToolUse reach the hook' question is PRE-ANSWERED as unverified — do NOT run Codex/Claude to confirm; the code defaults non-Pi to 'unverified' and the loop only delivers the testable reporting change.
steps: In src/bootstrap/setup-doctor.ts (+ the doctor --setup caller), change the capture-reach report from blanket-healthy to per-harness: Pi is bridge-checkable via ~/.pi allowlists; Codex/Claude are reported 'unverified / Pi-subagent-only'.
Default the non-Pi reach to 'unverified' from ADR-0043 context (no harness execution).
Add a test asserting doctor --setup output distinguishes Pi from non-Pi capture reach.
Record in this item's progress entry that non-Pi reach was pre-answered (unverified), not empirically run.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0124 Docs, metadata, and qmd collection-list parse cleanup (G12/G13 + distribution/metadata). No blockers. Does NOT touch the README [research] block (item 0119 owns that).
steps: package.json: rewrite the stale description ('locked Obsidian vault') to the current de-workflowed artifact-store framing; move version off 0.0.0.
README.md: replace the locked-doc-category language with the config-driven section/bucket model (ADR-0037/PRD-0019). Leave the [research] block to item 0119.

Harden qmd collection-list parsing (src/integrations/qmd.ts): prefer a `--json` collection-list form (or a version-robust parse) so a qmd output-format change does not masquerade as 'never synced'. Pin with a test.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0125 Skill->kind routing + draft-stamp authoring contract (ADR-0040 routing side). BLOCKED BY SLICE-0120 (capture G1 must land first). kindForSkill already exists; the real deliverables are the stamp-template contract doc and the reminder string change.
steps: Confirm the wiki.json `skill` field resolves a skill to its kind (kindForSkill in src/artifacts/registry.ts); pin the mapping with a test.
Document the stamp-`template:<kind>` (and `project:`) authoring contract in the bundled skill skills/wiki/SKILL.md.

Update the write/session-end hook guidance string (src/cli/verbs/hooks.ts STOP_REMINDER / hookGuidance) to name the stamp-template step, not only 'run wiki create'; assert the new wording in tests/cli-hook.test.ts.
Add an end-to-end test: a draft stamped per the contract is captured into the configured kind on a TEMP vault.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0126 Incremental keyword index update on every write (G4, ADR-0041 freshness side; amends PRD-0018). BLOCKED BY SLICE-0121 (lock). SHARED-SEAM RULE: put the qmd update INSIDE mintAndWrite in store.ts (same seam as the lock), NOT at each call site, so it covers create and capture without editing capture.ts. Soft-order after SLICE-0119.
steps: In mintAndWrite (src/artifacts/store.ts), after the write and inside the per-project lock from SLICE-0121, fire a cheap incremental qmd `update` (keyword index only) for the project's collection.
Keep vector `embed` owned solely by `wiki sync`; the write path must NOT embed.
Keep `wiki search` a pure read (no qmd update on the read path) — preserve PRD-0018's read-only contract.
Update PRD-0018's pinned search tests to the amended contract (search read-only, write path owns the keyword update) explicitly — do not delete them.
Verify after `wiki create` the new artifact is in the keyword index with no manual `wiki sync`; embed runs only on sync. Use a TEMP vault.
Run all three gates green and commit.

items.description: SLICE-0127 Capture runs the dedup gate and warns-and-files on a match (G6, ADR-0042). BLOCKED BY SLICE-0120 (capture G1) AND SLICE-0126 (so the dedup qmd refresh runs inside the lock, never unlocked). Capture must never block/prompt (non-interactive hook).
steps: Route the capture path through the same runDedupGate seam wiki create uses (src/artifacts/dedup.ts), inside the per-project locked section.
On a strong dedup match, FILE the artifact anyway and write 'possible duplicate of [[X]] — review' to stderr; never block, never prompt, never drop (including on a false-positive match).
Ensure the locked critical section order on the capture path is: dedup refresh+query -> allocate -> write -> qmd update, all inside the one per-project lock (no unlocked qmd touch).
Leave wiki create's existing advisory dedup behavior unchanged.
Tests use a TEMP vault; assert a strong-match capture both files and warns, and that nothing is dropped.
Run all three gates green and commit.
